description: A portable personal computer that integrates a display, keyboard, and trackpad in a compact form factor. Laptops are powered by rechargeable batteries and contain numerous sophisticated components working in concert to deliver computing power in a mobile package.
functionality: Processes data, runs software applications, enables communication, and stores information. The CPU executes instructions, RAM provides working memory, storage retains data persistently, and the GPU renders visuals.
interesting_facts: A modern laptop CPU contains billions of transistors etched at nanometer scale.
Laptops dissipate heat through copper heat pipes connected to small fans.
The first commercially available laptop was the Osborne 1, released in 1981.
Modern SSDs can read data at speeds exceeding 7,000 MB/s using NVMe protocol.
recyclability: Moderate - contains recoverable metals (gold, copper, aluminum) and hazardous materials requiring special handling.

components.short_description: The central processing unit that executes all program instructions.
Volatile memory that temporarily stores data actively used by the operating system and applications.
Solid-state drive that persistently stores the operating system, applications, and user data.
Rechargeable lithium-polymer or lithium-ion battery pack providing portable power.
Dedicated or integrated graphics processor responsible for rendering images and video.
The main circuit board connecting all components via buses, slots, and connectors.
